back_translation: The descendants of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, so the LORD delivered them into the hand of Midian for seven years.
The hand of Midian prevailed over Israel. Because of fear of the Midianites, the descendants of Israel made hideouts for themselves in the mountains, in caves, and in strongholds.
Whenever Israel planted crops, the Midianites, the Amalekites, and the people of the east would come up and invade them.
They encamped against the descendants of Israel and destroyed the produce of the land all the way to Gaza, leaving no sustenance in Israel — neither sheep nor oxen nor donkeys.
They came with their livestock and their tents like a great swarm of locusts. They and their camels were innumerable. They came into the land to destroy it.
Israel was greatly impoverished by Midian, and the descendants of Israel cried out to the LORD.
When the descendants of Israel cried out to the LORD because of Midian,
the LORD sent a prophet to the descendants of Israel, who said to them, 'Thus says the LORD God of Israel: I brought you up out of Egypt, and I brought you out of the house of slavery.'
'I delivered you from the hand of the Egyptians and from the hand of all your oppressors. I drove them out before you and gave you their land.'
'And I said to you: I am the LORD your God. You shall not fear the gods of the Amorites in whose land you dwell. But you have not obeyed my voice.'
Then the angel of the LORD came and sat under the oak at Ophrah that belonged to Joash the Abiezrite, while his son Gideon was threshing wheat in the winepress to hide from the Midianites.
The angel of the LORD appeared to him and said, 'The LORD is with you, O mighty man of valor.'

Gideon answered, 'Please, my lord, if the LORD is with us, why has all this happened to us? And where are all His wonders that our fathers told us about — saying, Did not the LORD bring us up from Egypt? But now the LORD has forsaken us and delivered us into the hand of Midian.'
The LORD turned to him and said, 'Go in this strength of yours and save Israel from the hand of Midian. Have I not sent you?'
Gideon answered, 'Please, my lord, how can I save Israel? Behold, my clan is the weakest in Manasseh, and I am the youngest in my father's house.'
The LORD said to him, 'Surely I will be with you, and you will strike down the Midianites as one man.'
Gideon said, 'If I have found favor in your sight, please give me a sign that it is you speaking with me.'
'Please do not depart from this place until I return to you and bring out my offering and place it before you.' He said, 'I will stay until you return.'
Gideon went in and prepared a young goat and unleavened bread from an ephah of flour. He placed the meat in a basket and the broth in a pot, brought them out to him under the oak, and presented them.
The angel of God said to him, 'Take the meat and the unleavened bread and lay them on this rock, and pour out the broth.' Gideon did so.

The angel of the LORD stretched out the tip of the staff that was in his hand and touched the meat and the unleavened bread. Fire flared up from the rock and consumed the meat and the unleavened bread. Then the angel of the LORD vanished from his sight.
Gideon realized that he was the angel of the LORD, and Gideon said, 'Alas, Lord GOD! I have seen the angel of the LORD face to face!'
But the LORD said to him, 'Peace be with you. Do not be afraid; you shall not die.'
So Gideon built an altar there to the LORD and called it Yahweh-Shalom (The LORD is Peace). To this day it still stands in Ophrah of the Abiezrites.
That very night the LORD said to him, 'Take your father's bull and the second bull of seven years old, and tear down your father's altar of Baal, and cut down the Asherah pole that is beside it.'
'Then build a proper altar to the LORD your God on the top of this stronghold, and with the wood of the Asherah pole that you cut down, offer the second bull as a burnt offering.'
Gideon took ten of his servants and did as the LORD had said to him. But because he feared his father's household and the men of the city, he did it at night rather than during the day.

When the men of the city got up in the morning, behold, the altar of Baal had been torn down, the Asherah pole beside it had been cut down, and the second bull had been offered as a burnt offering on the altar that was built.
They said to one another, 'Who did this?' When they investigated and inquired, they were told, 'Gideon son of Joash did this.'
The men of the city said to Joash, 'Bring out your son. He must die, because he has torn down the altar of Baal and cut down the Asherah pole beside it.'

But Joash said to all who stood against him, 'Will you contend for Baal? Will you save him? Whoever contends for him shall die by morning. If he is a god, let him contend for himself, because someone has torn down his altar.'
On that day they called Gideon 'Jerubbaal' — that is to say, 'Let Baal contend with him' — because he had torn down Baal's altar.
All the Midianites, the Amalekites, and the people of the east gathered together, crossed over the Jordan, and camped in the Valley of Jezreel.
The Spirit of the LORD clothed Gideon, and he blew the ram's horn, and the Abiezrites were called out to follow him.
Gideon sent messengers throughout Manasseh, who were also called out to follow him, and he sent messengers to Asher, Zebulun, and Naphtali, and they came up to meet them.
Gideon said to God, 'If you will save Israel by my hand as you have said,
'behold, I will place a wool fleece on the threshing floor. If there is dew only on the fleece and all the ground is dry, then I will know that you will save Israel by my hand, as you have said.'
And it was so. When Gideon arose the next morning, he wrung out the fleece and squeezed dew from it — a bowl full of water.

Then Gideon said to God, 'Do not let your anger burn against me; let me speak just this once more. Please let me test once more with the fleece. This time let only the fleece be dry, and let there be dew on all the ground.'
God did so that night. Only the fleece was dry, and dew was on all the ground.
